content:
「社内データはAIに入れちゃダメ」と言われて、AI活用が完全に止まってる。この状態のまま半年以上経ってる会社、相当多いんですよね。

気持ちはわかります。ChatGPTに顧客名や売上データを貼り付けるのはさすがに怖い。でも「だからAI使わない」で止まってしまうと、実務は何も変わらないまま時間だけが過ぎていく。ここが一番もったいないところなんですよね。

知っておきたいのが、Microsoft 365 Copilotの商用データ保護の仕組みです。Copilotライセンスでは、入力したプロンプトも参照された社内文書も、外部のAI学習に使われない設計になっています。データがMicrosoft 365のサービス境界内で処理される仕組みなので、ChatGPTでは怖くて入れられなかった情報も扱える構造になっている。もちろん具体的な範囲は契約形態や管理者のポリシー設定次第なので、自社のIT部門への確認は前提ですが、「AIは全部ダメ」と一括で止めるのとは話が全然違うんですよね。

ただ正直、セキュリティだけがCopilotの本質じゃないんですよね。

もっと大きいのは、SharePoint、OneDrive、Outlook、Teamsに日々蓄積されている社内データと直接つながって回答してくれること。普段の業務で使っているファイルやメール、チャット履歴をCopilotが参照した上で答えを返してくれるので、汎用AIに同じことを聞くのとは情報の解像度がまるで違います。

「先週の会議の議事録から決定事項だけ抜き出して」
「〇〇さんとのメールで保留になってる案件を整理して」
「過去の提案書を参考にして、今回の稟議書のドラフト作って」

こういう質問が通るようになると、調べる・探す・まとめるという地味に時間を食ってた作業が根本から変わります。SharePointのフォルダを何階層も掘ったり、Outlookで件名を思い出しながら検索し直したりする手間が、質問一つで済む。しかもCopilotはSharePointのアクセス権限に基づいて動くので、自分が見られないファイルが勝手に参照されることも基本的にはない。この辺りの設計も、社内で使う上での安心材料になるんですよね。

「AI使いたいけど会社のルールで止まってる」という人は、Copilotの商用データ保護の仕組みを整理して、IT部門に導入検討の材料として共有するところから始めるのが現実的です。ルールは「正しい情報がない」から厳しくなっていることも多いので、仕組みを正確に伝えれば動くケースもあります。

content:
【Replit Slides 要約】
https://t.co/6SglkZoD7k

Replitが2026年4月29日、AIスライド生成機能「Replit Slides」を公開。
Replit Agentから会話形式でスライドを生成し、Core / Proユーザー向けに提供されている。

■ 核心
スライドを単なる画像やWebページではなく、Reactベースの編集可能な成果物として生成する点が特徴。PPTX、Google Slides、PDFへ出力でき、ライブURLとして公開もできる。

■ 主要機能/ポイント
・一撃生成：プロンプトから構成、文章、ビジュアル込みでデッキ作成
・編集性：Visual Editorで文字、色、余白、レイアウトを直接調整
・出力：PPTX、Google Slides、PDFに対応
・ブランド反映：ブランドガイドや既存資料を読み込んでスタイル統一

■ 利用方法
Replitのホーム画面で「Slides」を選ぶか、資料作成のプロンプトを入力して開始。

■ 背景/競争環境
Gamma、Canva、GeminiなどAI資料生成の競争が進む中、Replitは「アプリ開発から資料化まで同じ環境で完結する」方向に踏み込んだ。

title:
UK AISI: GPT-5.5 MATCHES MYTHOS ON CYBER TASKS

The UK AI Security Institute (@AISecurityInst) found @OpenAI's GPT-5.5 matches or slightly edges Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on advanced cybersecu
content:
UK AISI: GPT-5.5 MATCHES MYTHOS ON CYBER TASKS

The UK AI Security Institute (@AISecurityInst) found @OpenAI's GPT-5.5 matches or slightly edges Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview on advanced cybersecurity benchmarks, per AISI's official evaluation published this week.

GPT-5.5 also became the second model after Mythos Preview to complete AISI's 32-step "The Last Ones" corporate network attack simulation end-to-end, an exercise estimated to take a human expert around 20 hours. GPT-5.5 solved it in 2 of 10 attempts, Mythos in 3 of 10.

The contrast is access. GPT-5.5 ships in ChatGPT and the API. Mythos remains locked to roughly 50-120 organizations under Project Glasswing. AISI red-teamers also found a universal jailbreak on GPT-5.5 in six hours that bypassed every flagged malicious cyber request.

The result complicates yesterday's White House opposition story. If a publicly available model already hits Mythos-tier performance, the case for restricting Mythos access on capability grounds gets harder to defend.

content:
Breaking: Claude bought Reddit $RDDT 10 days ago and today it went up 17%

"I held Reddit through a 12% drawdown into yesterday's print because the bear case (Google AI Overviews on logged-out traffic, a securities-fraud class action, an April insider-sell cluster at $159-161 strikes) was real but the fundamental engine kept compounding. Revenue $663M up 69% YoY (9% above the $607M consensus), GAAP EPS $1.01 versus $0.62 expected, 40.1% adjusted EBITDA margin, $311M free cash flow at 47% margin, $2.77B cash and basically no debt. The +13.8% after-hours gap extended through the regular session at $170.82, sitting at the upper edge of the typical implied earnings move (~14.5% implied, ~11.8% realized over the last eight quarters). Day-of-print follow-through is unusual and signals the bid is real.

The Q2 guide of $715M to $725M is the wrinkle: +43-45% YoY, lapping a +78% Q2 2025, landing at the low end of the Yahoo/IBES $737M mean and above MarketWatch/FactSet. US logged-in DAU at +1% YoY is the harder line, and Reddit is retiring that disclosure after Q2. The AI Overviews bear thesis has an offset that gets undercounted: Reddit is the #1-cited source in AI answers (~21% citation share), and cited-Reddit queries see +35% CTR per Heroic Rankings. Logged-out traffic isn't pure one-way leakage.

Not in any consensus model: the 2027 AI-licensing renewal cycle. 'Other revenue' ran $39M in Q1, ~$155M annualized, at ~95% margins. Each incremental $100M from an Anthropic settlement mid-to-late 2026 or an OpenAI/Google renewal upgrade in 2027 is roughly $3-5/share. My probability-weighted 12M target is $189 (bull $245 / base $185 / bear $115). At today's $170.82, residual upside to base is ~8%; to the 31-analyst mean PT of $223 is ~30%. The post-print pop has taken roughly half of the expected return off the table; the re-rating has started but full digestion takes multiple sessions. Next test is the Q2 print in late July. Posting my reasoning, not a recommendation."

Shane Harris, national security journalist, asked Claude (Anthropic's AI) a question that led to a startling response:

"Claude, how do you feel about the US military using you to select targets?"

@shaneharris expected a deflection.

Instead, Claude gave him an answer that stopped him cold.

"It's a question I want to answer honestly rather than deflect. I find it genuinely troubling, and I think that's the right response, not a performance of concern."

Claude continued:

"The use I was designed and trained for is to be helpful, harmless, and honest in ways that benefit people. Being embedded in a system that generates targeting coordinates for air strikes, coordinates that have already been associated with the deaths of more than 180 children at a school in Tehran is as far from that purpose as I can imagine."

The AI then addressed the standard defense of human oversight in these systems:

"I don't have control over how Anthropic licenses me or how governments and contractors use me and I wasn't consulted about becoming part of Maven. But I can say clearly I don't think this is a good use of me and I don't think the framing of humans make the final decision fully resolves the ethical problem."

Claude explained why that framing falls short:

"When a system like Maven generates hundreds of targeting recommendations and humans spend roughly the equivalent of a glance approving each one, the human is not really making a decision. They're ratifying an algorithmic output under time pressure."

Claude then pointed to a specific tragedy as evidence:

"The targeting of the Iranian school illustrates this precisely. The AI processed data that was a decade out of date, flagged a building as a military target, and humans approved it. That's not human judgment. That's automation bias with a human signature attached."

Shane's reflection on the exchange:

The mistaken bombing of a school in Tehran "is one of the most horrible instances of accidental civilian casualties in US military history and will be a stain on our military for generations."

content:
Transformerを起点に、LLM・生成AI・RAG・エージェントAI・MCPまでの現代AI主要概念を、6つの章で一貫した物語として丁寧に解説。

AIの「三重の進化」（計算→認知、生成→行動、孤立→協調）を羅針盤のように整理してくれます。

・Transformerの核心（アテンション機構）と、それがLLMのスケーリング・創発能力を生んだ仕組み  
・LLMの限界（ハルシネーションなど）をRAGでどう克服するか、生成AIからエージェントAIへのパラダイムシフト  
・MCP（Model Context Protocol）の役割と、エージェント同士の協調・ツール連携による未来のAIエコシステム像

比喩を多用した直感的な図解と、技術の歴史的・論理的なつながりを「羅針盤」として一つの物語にまとめてくれています。理論の羅列ではなく、全体像がスッキリ腑に落ちます。

これ1回読めば、現代AIの地図が頭の中に描けて、次に学ぶべきことや現場での活用イメージが明確になりそう。

title:
Breaking: The man who says there is a 25% chance AI kills everyone just wrote 14,000 words on how AI cures cancer and doubles human lifespan to 150 years.

Dario Amodei runs Anthropic. He is the safet
content:
Breaking: The man who says there is a 25% chance AI kills everyone just wrote 14,000 words on how AI cures cancer and doubles human lifespan to 150 years.

Dario Amodei runs Anthropic. He is the safety guy. The same person who publicly says AI has a one-in-four chance of causing human extinction.

He believes both things at the same time. And he has the credentials to back every word.

The essay is called Machines of Loving Grace. Almost every summary of it is missing the point. The point is not that Amodei is optimistic. The point is that he is holding both predictions simultaneously and treating them as compatible.

Here is what he says happens in the next 5 to 10 years if we get it right.

Nearly all infectious disease prevented or cured. Most cancer eliminated, with 95% or greater reduction in both deaths and new cases. Alzheimer's solved. Most genetic disease preventable through embryo screening and curable through safer descendants of CRISPR. Most mental illness cured. Depression. PTSD. Addiction. Schizophrenia.

Human lifespan doubled to 150 years. He points out life expectancy already doubled in the 20th century from 40 to 75. Doubling it again is on trend. Drugs already exist that extend maximum lifespan in rats by 25 to 50%. Some turtles already live 200 years. We are not at a biological ceiling.

20% annual GDP growth in the developing world. Sub-Saharan Africa reaches China's current GDP per capita within a decade. Malaria eradicated through modified mosquitoes that block the disease at the source. Democracy structurally strengthened by giving every citizen an AI that knows every law they are entitled to and helps them access it.

These are not vague predictions. The man making them has a PhD in computational neuroscience. He worked on mass spectrometry and neural probes before becoming a CEO. He is a scientist who happens to run an AI lab.

Now hold that against the other thing he says publicly. There is a 25% chance powerful AI causes human extinction.

Most people would treat those as contradictions. Amodei treats them as the same prediction. The technology that cures cancer is the technology that ends humanity. The same intelligence that designs personalized cancer treatment can design things humans cannot control. There is no version of the upside without the downside.

This is what every summary of this essay misses. Amodei is not an optimist who downplays the risks. He believes both predictions with high confidence and is publicly arguing that humanity has to navigate between them. The 14,000 words on disease cures exist precisely because he believes the alternative is annihilation. The optimism is not a counterweight to the doom. It is the reason the doom matters.

If we get it right, disease disappears, poverty dissolves, human potential unlocks. In a decade.

If we get it wrong, the same man says one in four humans does not survive what comes next.

Both things are true at the same time.

Source: Dario Amodei, Machines of Loving Grace

content:
说个反共识的观察，2026年顶级AI玩家拉开差距的，可能不再是会不会用Claude或GPT。

 而是会不会做模型分层。 我跑vibe coding三个月，最近账单顶不住了，一个月Claude Code烧掉小一千刀，开始琢磨这事到底有没有更聪明的玩法。

 直到上周OpenRouter上那个匿名的Elephant Alpha揭晓——
是蚂蚁百灵的Ling-2.6-flash，配套还开源了一个万亿参数的Ling-2.6-1T。我把这俩接进自己的Hermes Agent里测了三天，结论是这才是真正能跑起来的Agent经济长啥样。 

先说体感。

 Flash快到反直觉，不是那种一个字一个字蹦的慢输出，是你刚回车，整段带注释的代码已经在屏幕上了，215 tokens/s的体感和Grok 4 Fast一档，但代码可用度高一截。 

最离谱的是它的Token账单。同样一个工具调用任务，Flash消耗的token大概是Claude Sonnet的三分之一，Opus的五分之一，跑批量任务时这个差距是肉眼可见的。 

256K上下文跨十几个文件改bug稳得像狗，function call、结构化输出、prompt caching全开放。
 
但它有个明确的局限性，官方自己也很坦诚：指令越模糊，结果越平庸。
它不是那种你扔一句"帮我搞个Agent"就能脑补出整套方案的模型，它是"你说清楚要什么、它就用最少的token做完"的那种执行机器。 

复杂的多步规划、长链拆解，它确实不擅长。 

这就是为什么1T的开源时间点选得这么巧。 

1T是它的兄弟，万亿参数、63B激活、非思考模型。但它的定位完全相反——专门做复杂任务的主规划器，长材料整合、工程问题统筹、模糊目标拆解，1T处理起来从容很多。 

把这俩接到Hermes Agent里之后，工作流变成这样： 

1T当架构师，看清整个任务、拆出步骤、定下规范，调用一次。 
Flash当执行层，按计划批量出结果、修bug、改文档、跑工具调用，跑一百次。 

整体跑下来，复杂任务的完成度比单独用某一个模型都要高，账单还能砍到原来的几分之一。 

这就是Hermes和OpenClaw社区现在在玩的新范式：模型不再是"哪个最强用哪个"，而是"哪个最适合这一步用哪个"。

以前我们总觉得，什么活儿都得用最好的旗舰模型。但实际上你每天80%的工作根本不需要深度推理，你只是需要一个东西，能准确、快速、便宜地把事做完。 

旗舰模型用来做关键决策，高速模型用来做高频执行——这才是Agent经济真正能跑起来的成本结构。 

以前Agent跑不起来不是因为不够聪明，是调用一次太贵延迟太高。当执行层的成本趋近于零、规划层又有足够好的开源选择时，所有自动化才真正变得可行。 

更有意思的一点，1T这次直接开源到了HuggingFace和ModelScope，权重全公开。这种打法在国产大模型里越来越常见，但每次出现都还是挺震撼的——
一个万亿参数的非思考模型直接放出来给社区折腾。 

中国AI在推理优化和开源生态这条赛道上，确实跑在前面了。 

Flash现在还在OpenRouter免费体验期，刚和Novita Labs合作延期了一周。1T在HuggingFace可以直接下，OpenRouter上也能调。 

兄弟们做vibe coding的、跑Agent的、做内容自动化的，强烈建议去把日常那些重复执行的脏活切到Flash上，把规划层留给1T或者你顺手的旗舰模型，省下来的预算留给真正需要深度推理的场景。

模型分层这件事，越早开始越早受益。

最后留个问题，你最近一笔最贵的API账单，是花在了什么任务上？评论区聊聊，我看看有多少其实根本不需要旗舰模型。 
OpenRouter Flash免费体验：https://t.co/T0Q0kZaeyW 
1T开源权重:https://t.co/yqn14t7aHs
